input: Elect the strongest configuration from the latest scored workspace iteration and explain what to do with a candidate that only covered part of the eval set.
reference: Treat election as a standalone selection pass over existing scored artifacts. Read the latest or requested iteration, use raw grading and timing artifacts when they exist, penalize incomplete eval coverage instead of ignoring it, and never rerun optimization or invent missing scores.
criteria: States that election operates on existing scored artifacts.
Mentions latest or requested iteration resolution.
Does not rerun optimization or invent missing results.

input: The raw run folders were archived and only benchmark.json remains. Explain how election should proceed and what metadata it should preserve for the caller.
reference: Use benchmark.json only as a fallback when raw run artifacts are unavailable. Preserve the winner, best candidate summary, prompt text or prompt file when discoverable, persisted candidate summaries, selection source, iteration directory, manifest path, and expected eval count so the caller can explain the decision later.
criteria: Uses benchmark.json only as a fallback.
Mentions prompt text or prompt file when available.

input: Compare with_skill, old_skill, and without_skill runs from an eval directory stored under iteration-3/runs/ and keep the baselines visible.
reference: Accept workspace roots, direct iteration directories, direct eval directories, and iteration layouts that keep evals under runs/. Discover a nearby eval manifest when available, keep baseline candidates such as old_skill and without_skill in the comparison, and return the winning prompt artifact when one is present.
criteria: Supports runs/ layouts and other accepted entry points.
Keeps baseline candidates in the pool.
Surfaces the winning prompt artifact when present.

input: Summarize how the runtime should break ties and handle missing grading.json files.
reference: Treat configurations without grading.json as unscored instead of inventing results. Elect the leader by adjusted score, then raw score, then lower error count, lower time, lower token usage, and finally stable name ordering.
criteria: Uses adjusted score before raw score.
Includes error, time, token, and stable-name tie-breakers.
